title: QA: BACKGROUND_SHELLS is process-global LazyLock singleton (DIP violation)
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
decoupling
## Location
src/tools/bash/mod.rs:271-272
## Violation
`BACKGROUND_SHELLS` is a process-wide `LazyLock<BackgroundShellManager>`. Tests share state, multi-tenant deployments cannot isolate per-session shells, bash module cannot be unit-tested without real processes. Textbook DIP violation.

Quoted (mod.rs:270-272):
```
/// Global background shell manager
pub static BACKGROUND_SHELLS: std::sync::LazyLock<BackgroundShellManager> =
    std::sync::LazyLock::new(BackgroundShellManager::new);
```

Pinning tests admit the problem ("Because the global `BACKGROUND_SHELLS` is shared across the test binary, this test might interact with others" — mod.rs:479-482).
## Mandated refactor
1. Introduce `pub trait ShellManager: Send + Sync`.
2. Plumb `Arc<dyn ShellManager>` through tool dispatch context.
3. Eliminate the `pub static` once injection is in place.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
